# agenticow

> Branch your agent's memory like Git — 0.5 ms, 162 bytes, any base size.

agenticow branches a vector memory copy-on-write, like Git: fork a million-vector base in ~0.5 ms and 162 bytes, query parent ∪ your edits, roll back instant…

This is an independent, art-directed explainer for ruvnet's agenticow — it takes a newcomer from "never seen it" to "ready to implement", grounded in a real RVF knowledge base of the source.

## What it is
agenticow: A luminous version-control graph grown from living memory — a bright base "trunk" of vectors that forks into a fan of gossamer-thin branches, each a near-weightless copy-on-write delta. Git's branch/checkpoint/rollback graph, rendered as a bioluminescent root system.

## For your AI
A downloadable knowledge pack (RVF vector KB + MCP server) ships with this page so your agent can search the source too: https://agenticow-explainer.netlify.app/agenticow-knowledge-pack.zip

## Links
- Live explainer: https://agenticow-explainer.netlify.app/
- Source repository: https://github.com/ruvnet/agenticow
- AI knowledge pack: https://agenticow-explainer.netlify.app/agenticow-knowledge-pack.zip
